commands.review_summary:
This PR updates the iOS Live Activity manager, widget UI, and gateway model wiring to end stale or no-longer-useful activities, prune old activities during hydration, show attention states, and use compact status icons.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection, not from a live device run in this review. Current main can start or update a Connecting Live Activity before node gateway connection and does not end it when the node connection reaches a healthy state.
commands.review_followup: No repair lane is needed from this read-only review; this active maintainer PR has no discrete blocking finding for automation to fix.

target.title: fix(ios): improve live activity lifecycle
target.body:
## Summary
- end the iOS Live Activity when OpenClaw is connected, idle, manually disconnected, or otherwise no longer useful
- add stale/duplicate cleanup during Live Activity hydration so old connecting activities do not linger
- replace the oversized progress UI with compact status icons and clearer lock screen/Dynamic Island states
- surface paused/approval-needed sessions as attention states instead of indefinite connecting states

## Verification
- `xcodebuild -project apps/ios/OpenClaw.xcodeproj -scheme OpenClaw -destination 'generic/platform=iOS Simulator' build`
- `git diff --check`
- `xcodebuild -project apps/ios/OpenClaw.xcodeproj -scheme OpenClaw -destination 'id=00008140-000848A92EE3001C' -derivedDataPath /tmp/openclaw-ios-device-build build`
- `xcrun devicectl device install app --device 6A19D82B-4EA6-5D67-B3A7-0AB3B71B550C /tmp/openclaw-ios-device-build/Build/Products/Debug-iphoneos/OpenClaw.app`
- `xcrun devicectl device process launch --device 6A19D82B-4EA6-5D67-B3A7-0AB3B71B550C ai.openclaw.ios.test.guti-gzs353x62e`

Behavior addressed: OpenClaw no longer leaves a stale Live Activity stuck in Connecting after the app reaches a healthy connected/idle state, and approval-paused sessions get a distinct attention state.
Real environment tested: iOS Simulator generic build plus Nimrod's physical iPhone 16 Pro via devicectl.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: Built the app for simulator, checked whitespace, built for the physical iPhone, installed the signed Debug-iphoneos app, and launched bundle `ai.openclaw.ios.test.guti-gzs353x62e` on the device.
Evidence after fix: Device build, install, and launch all completed successfully; the installed app bundle was reported at `/private/var/containers/Bundle/Application/6ED54CE7-140F-47D4-9F0E-7504D1D0EC96/OpenClaw.app/`.
Observed result after fix: The patched OpenClaw app launched on the physical phone with the new Live Activity lifecycle code included.
What was not tested: I did not capture a post-patch lock screen screenshot while driving a live gateway session through each Live Activity state. The autoreview helper was attempted with `AUTOREVIEW_AUTO_TESTS=0 .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`, but the nested Codex review process was killed by the OS before returning findings.
target.author: ngutman
